quiz.title: Unit 6 Quiz 9, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Chemical Reactions Review
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Types of Chemical Reactions, Products of Reactions, Reaction Classification

questions.question: When $\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3$ reacts with HCl, the products are:
questions.explanation: Sodium carbonate reacts with hydrochloric acid to produce sodium chloride, carbon dioxide, and water: $\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3 + 2\text{HCl} \rightarrow 2\text{NaCl} + \text{CO}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$.

questions.question: In the equation $2\text{Al}(s) + 3\text{Fe(NO}_3)_2(aq) \rightarrow 3\text{Fe}(s) + 2\text{Al(NO}_3)_3(aq)$, iron has been replaced by:
questions.explanation: In this single displacement reaction, aluminum replaces iron in iron(II) nitrate. Aluminum is more reactive than iron, so it displaces iron from the compound, forming aluminum nitrate and solid iron. Therefore, iron has been replaced by aluminum.

questions.question: In a double-replacement reaction, hydrogen chloride and sodium hydroxide react to produce sodium chloride. Another product is:
questions.explanation: Hydrogen chloride (HCl) and sodium hydroxide (NaOH) undergo a double-replacement (acid-base neutralization) reaction. The H⁺ from HCl combines with the OH⁻ from NaOH to form water (H₂O). The balanced equation is: $\text{HCl} + \text{NaOH} \rightarrow \text{NaCl} + \text{H}_2\text{O}$.

questions.question:
$\text{CS}_2 + 3\text{O}_2 \rightarrow \text{CO}_2 + 2\text{SO}_2$

This reaction is an example of:
questions.explanation: This is a combustion reaction. Carbon disulfide ($\text{CS}_2$) burns in oxygen to produce carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide. Combustion reactions are characterized by a substance reacting with oxygen, often releasing heat and light. The products are typically oxides of the elements present in the fuel.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a true statement concerning what happens in all chemical reactions?
options: The ways in which atoms are joined together are changed.
New atoms are formed as products.
The starting materials are named reactants.
The bonds of the reactants are broken and new bonds of the products are formed.
questions.explanation: Statement B is NOT true. In chemical reactions, atoms are rearranged, but no new atoms are created or destroyed. This is the Law of Conservation of Mass. The other statements are true: A (chemical bonds are rearranged), C (reactants are the starting materials), and D (bonds are broken and formed in reactions).

questions.question: A skeleton equation does NOT show which of the following?
options: The correct formulas of the reactants and products
The reactants on the left, the products on the right
An arrow connecting the reactants to the products
The relative amounts of reactants and products
questions.explanation: A skeleton equation shows the correct formulas of reactants and products, with reactants on the left and products on the right, connected by an arrow. However, it does NOT show the relative amounts (coefficients) of reactants and products. The physical states (s, l, g, aq) may or may not be included, but the key missing element in a skeleton equation is the balancing coefficients.

questions.question: Chemical equations must be balanced to satisfy the _____.
questions.explanation: Chemical equations must be balanced to satisfy the Law of Conservation of Mass, which states that matter cannot be created or destroyed in a chemical reaction. Therefore, the number of atoms of each element must be the same on both sides of the equation. The Law of Definite Proportions (A) states that a compound always contains the same proportion of elements by mass. The Law of Multiple Proportions (B) relates to ratios of elements in different compounds. Avogadro's principle (D) deals with volumes of gases.

questions.question: In the chemical equation, $\text{H}_2\text{O}_2(aq) \rightarrow \text{H}_2\text{O}(l) + \text{O}_2(g)$, the $\text{H}_2\text{O}_2$ is a _____.
questions.explanation: In this chemical equation, $\text{H}_2\text{O}_2$ (hydrogen peroxide) is written on the left side of the arrow, indicating it is a reactant. Reactants are the starting materials that undergo change during a chemical reaction. Products (A) appear on the right side. A catalyst (C) speeds up a reaction without being consumed. The (aq) indicates it's aqueous, but it's not a gas (D).

questions.question: A catalyst is _____.
options: One of the reactants in single-replacement reactions
A solid product of a reaction
A chemical that speeds up the reaction
questions.explanation: A catalyst is a substance that increases the rate of a chemical reaction without being consumed or permanently changed in the process. It works by lowering the activation energy. Catalysts are not products (A), not reactants in single-replacement reactions (B), and not necessarily solid products (C).

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{KClO}_3(s) \rightarrow \text{KCl}(s) + \text{O}_2(g)$, the coefficient of $\text{KClO}_3$ is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $2\text{KClO}_3 \rightarrow 2\text{KCl} + 3\text{O}_2$. To balance, start with KClO₃: left has 1 K, 1 Cl, 3 O; right has 1 K, 1 Cl, 2 O. To balance oxygen, use 2 KClO₃ (6 O) producing 3 O₂ (6 O). This gives 2 K and 2 Cl on left, so need 2 KCl on right. Therefore, the coefficient of KClO₃ is 2.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{C}_2\text{H}_6 + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow \text{CO}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$, the coefficient of $\text{O}_2$ is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $2\text{C}_2\text{H}_6 + 7\text{O}_2 \rightarrow 4\text{CO}_2 + 6\text{H}_2\text{O}$. To balance, start with C₂H₆: 2 C and 6 H. Place coefficient 2 before C₂H₆ to get whole numbers: $2\text{C}_2\text{H}_6$ gives 4 C and 12 H. Then 4 CO₂ (4 C) and 6 H₂O (12 H). Oxygen: 4 CO₂ has 8 O, 6 H₂O has 6 O, total 14 O atoms on right, so need 7 O₂ molecules on left. Therefore, the coefficient of O₂ is 7.

questions.question: When the following equation is balanced, $\text{Mg} + \text{HCl} \rightarrow \text{MgCl}_2 + \text{H}_2$, the coefficient of HCl is _____.
questions.explanation: The balanced equation is $\text{Mg} + 2\text{HCl} \rightarrow \text{MgCl}_2 + \text{H}_2$. To balance, Mg is already balanced (1 on each side). Chlorine: right side has 2 Cl in MgCl₂, so left side needs 2 HCl. Hydrogen: left side has 2 H from 2 HCl, right side has 2 H in H₂. Therefore, the coefficient of HCl is 2.

questions.question: The reaction $2\text{HgO}(s) \rightarrow 2\text{Hg}(l) + \text{O}_2(g)$ is a(n):
questions.explanation: This is a decomposition reaction. A single compound (mercury(II) oxide) breaks down into two simpler substances (mercury metal and oxygen gas). Decomposition reactions have the general form: $\text{AB} \rightarrow \text{A} + \text{B}$.

questions.question: The reaction $2\text{Mg}(s) + \text{O}_2(g) \rightarrow 2\text{MgO}(s)$ is a:
questions.explanation: This is a synthesis reaction (also called a combination reaction). Two or more reactants (magnesium and oxygen) combine to form a single product (magnesium oxide). Synthesis reactions have the general form: $\text{A} + \text{B} \rightarrow \text{AB}$.

questions.question: In the reaction $\text{Zn} + \text{CuSO}_4 \rightarrow \text{ZnSO}_4 + \text{Cu}$, zinc is:
questions.explanation: Zinc loses electrons and is oxidized in this reaction, but since we're removing redox terminology, this question should be replaced or rephrased.

scoreMessages.70: Good job! Keep practicing reaction types.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Review the different reaction classifications.
scoreMessages.0: Please review the basics of chemical reactions.
